quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 6 Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Base Angles, Diagonals, and the Midsegment
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 6.6 Properties of Trapezoids and Isosceles Trapezoids

questions.question: A trapezoid is defined as a quadrilateral with:
options: Exactly one pair of opposite sides that are parallel
Both pairs of opposite sides that are parallel
All four sides that are congruent in length
Diagonals that are perpendicular to each other
questions.explanation: A trapezoid is a quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides. The parallel sides are called bases, and the non-parallel sides are called legs.

questions.question: An isosceles trapezoid is a trapezoid with:
questions.explanation: An isosceles trapezoid has congruent legs (the non-parallel sides). This gives it additional properties not shared by all trapezoids.

questions.question: Which statement is true about the base angles of an isosceles trapezoid?
options: Each pair of base angles is congruent
Base angles are always right angles
questions.explanation: In an isosceles trapezoid, the base angles adjacent to each base are congruent. For example, the two angles on one base are equal to each other.

questions.question: Which statement is true about the diagonals of an isosceles trapezoid?
questions.explanation: The diagonals of an isosceles trapezoid are congruent. This distinguishes them from general trapezoids, which do not have congruent diagonals.

questions.question: The midsegment of a trapezoid connects:
options: A vertex to the midpoint of a leg
The intersection of the diagonals to a base
questions.explanation: The midsegment (or median) of a trapezoid connects the midpoints of the two legs. It is parallel to both bases.

questions.question: The length of the midsegment of a trapezoid is:
options: The average of the lengths of the two bases
Equal to the longer base structural line segment
Half the sum of the non-parallel outer legs
The absolute difference of the two base metrics
questions.explanation: The midsegment of a trapezoid has length equal to the average of the two bases: $\frac{b_1 + b_2}{2}$. It is parallel to both bases.

questions.question: A trapezoid has bases of length $12$ and $20$. What is the length of its midsegment?
questions.explanation: The midsegment length is the average of the bases: $\frac{12 + 20}{2} = \frac{32}{2} = 16$.

questions.question: In isosceles trapezoid $ABCD$ with $\overline{AB} \parallel \overline{CD}$, $m\angle A = 65^\circ$. What is $m\angle D$?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles trapezoid, base angles on the same base are congruent. $\angle A$ and $\angle D$ are base angles on base $\overline{AD}$, so $m\angle D = m\angle A = 65^\circ$.

questions.question: In isosceles trapezoid $PQRS$ with $\overline{PQ} \parallel \overline{SR}$, $m\angle P = 70^\circ$. What is $m\angle Q$?
questions.explanation: $\angle P$ and $\angle Q$ are base angles on the same base $\overline{PQ}$. In an isosceles trapezoid, base angles on the same base are congruent, so $m\angle Q = 70^\circ$.

questions.question: A trapezoid has a midsegment of length $15$. If one base is $11$, what is the length of the other base?
questions.explanation: The midsegment formula is $m = \frac{b_1 + b_2}{2}$. Substituting: $15 = \frac{11 + b_2}{2}$, so $30 = 11 + b_2$, giving $b_2 = 19$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the properties of trapezoids and isosceles trapezoids, including base angles, diagonals, and the midsegment formula.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main properties but review the midsegment formula: $m = \frac{b_1 + b_2}{2}$.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: isosceles trapezoids have congruent legs, congruent base angles on each base, and congruent diagonals. The midsegment is parallel to the bases and equals their average.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides. In an isosceles trapezoid, legs are congruent, base angles are congruent, and diagonals are congruent. Midsegment = average of the bases.
